description: A logistics-master record for a warehouse / store / site (e.g. Barka, Vadi Kabir). The code is the natural key; a requisition line and goods receipt reference a warehouse for stock location and the receiving destination. Maintainable individually or by bulk import (natural-key upsert by code).

fields.name: code
fields.label: Warehouse Code
fields.validation: non-blank; uppercase; natural primary key; unique; immutable; the bulk-import upsert key
fields.purpose: The stable code a requisition line / goods receipt references as the stock location and receiving destination; the natural key bulk import upserts on.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Warehouse Name
fields.validation: non-blank; the display field for the warehouse reference dropdown
fields.purpose: The human-readable name shown in warehouse dropdowns so a requester/receiver picks a store by name; the display field for the reference.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Site / Location
fields.validation: non-blank; the site/store e.g. Barka, Vadi Kabir, Muscat
fields.purpose: The physical site the warehouse sits at; drives the delivery-place default on POs and the site-conditional approval stage (e.g. a factory site needs the Factory Manager stage).

fields.name: type
fields.label: Type
fields.validation: one of the set; BONDED for customs-bonded import storage
fields.purpose: Classifies the warehouse so import flows can route to a bonded store and factory sites trigger their conditional approval stage; informational for routing and inbound logistics.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: INACTIVE not selectable on new lines/receipts; existing records retain their warehouse
fields.purpose: Lets an admin retire a closed warehouse from new use without altering lines or receipts that reference it.

invariants: A13 audit + SSE on every committed master change
code is the natural key; the bulk-import upsert key
